pattern: Find the global maximum once, then a single pass checks whether each element plus the bonus reaches that ceiling.
hints: You need to know the current maximum across all kids before you can decide who qualifies — compute it first.
After one extra scan, kid i qualifies if candies[i] + extraCandies is at least the global max.
A single precomputed max lets you answer every kid in O(1), giving an overall O(n) solution with O(1) extra space.

edge_inputs.description: all kids tie for the maximum
single dominant kid, extras too small to bridge any gap
all values at constraint ceiling, extra at ceiling
extra exactly bridges the gap for some but not all
only the already-maximum kid qualifies when extra is 1

stress.description: 100-element random candy array with moderate extras
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    candies = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    extra = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [candies, extra]
